retry_reason: The kref_init call unconditionally succeeds; any failure (e.g., allocation, idr) is not a refcount failure and retrying would require freeing resources, not reattempting the refcount operation.
contract_summary: alloc_drm_dp_aux_dev is a set wrapper (constructor) that allocates a drm_dp_aux_dev, initializes its internal reference count to 1 via kref_init, and registers it in the aux_idr. The caller receives ownership of the newly created object with a single reference. On success, the caller must eventually release this reference by calling a corresponding put (e.g., kref_put or the appropriate drm_dp_aux_dev_put). On failure (memory allocation or IDR exhaustion), the function returns an ERR_PTR and the caller should not attempt to use the object.
